text:
MEDICORE CLINIC
Management System
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Complete Software Requirements Specification
Implementation Plan  •  SRS Document  •  Workflow Examples
Data Flow Diagrams  •  Entity Relationship Diagrams  •  Agile Roadmap
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Table of Contents
1. Executive Summary
MediCore Clinic Management System is a comprehensive, cloud-ready web application designed to streamline operations for both Homeopathy and Allopathy clinics. The system addresses t

he complete patient journey — from appointment booking through treatment, digital prescription, billing, and post-visit follow-up — while also providing income tracking and management dashboards for clinic owners.
The system supports role-based access (Receptionist and Doctor), multi-language interfaces, and specialty-specific workflows. Homeopathy doctors get structured case paper forms with potency tracking; Allopathy doctors get digital prescription pads and procedure-based billing. Both spec

ialties benefit from an intelligent queue management system that ensures appointment-holders are prioritized over walk-ins.
2. Implementation Plan
2.1 Technology Stack
2.2 System Architecture
The system follows a clean three-tier architecture:
2.3 Development Phases (Agile Sprints)
3. Software Requirements Specification (SRS)
3.1 System Scope
MediCore serves clinics with two specialties: Homeopathy and Allopathy. The specialty is set at doctor registration and determines which prescription inter

face and case paper forms are available. A single clinic instance can have one or more doctors and one or more receptionists.
3.2 User Roles
3.3 Functional Requirements
FR-01: User Registration & Authentication
Doctor registers with: name, speciality (Homeopathy / Allopathy), clinic name, phone, email, license number, language preference
Receptionist account created by doctor/admin with limited privileges
Login via email + password; JWT tokens with 8-hour access and 30-day refresh
Role-based rou

te guards on frontend; role-checked middleware on backend
Password reset via OTP to registered mobile number
FR-02: Patient Registration & Management
Fields: Full name, date of birth, gender, mobile number (primary key for lookup), address, blood group, known allergies, chronic conditions
Auto-check for existing patient by mobile number before creating new record
Patient search: by name, mobile, patient ID
Patient history: chronological list of all visits, diagnoses, prescriptions, payments, fol

low-ups
Potency history (Homeopathy): track every medicine given with potency, dose, duration, and response
FR-03: Appointment Booking
Patients can book online (future portal) or receptionist books on their behalf
Slot selection: date + time (based on doctor's availability schedule)
Walk-in registration: patient added to queue after all booked appointments for that slot
Appointment confirmation: SMS/WhatsApp with date, time, doctor name, clinic address
Appointment statuses: Scheduled → Checked-I

n → In-Treatment → Completed → No-Show → Cancelled
FR-04: Queue Management (Real-Time)

Receptionist screen: live queue board showing all patients for the day with status colour coding
Receptionist can: mark patient as Checked-In, skip (No-Show), re-order walk-ins
Doctor screen: shows ONLY the currently In-Treatment patient's full record
Doctor presses 'Next Patient' → current patient moves to Completed → next Checked-In patient moves to In-Treatment
WebSocket push: when receptionist changes status, doctor's screen updates instantly without page refresh
Priority enforcement: booked

appointments before walk-ins in the same slot window
FR-05: Homeopathy Case Paper
Structured digital form with the following sections:
Chief Complaint: free text + dropdown (duration, onset, character)
Modalities: multiple-choice (better from: warmth/cold/motion/rest; worse from: same options)
Mental/Emotional State: dropdowns (anxiety type, irritability level, grief history, dreams)
Physical Generals: thermal reaction (chilly/hot/warm), perspiration (part, character, smell), appetite, thirst,

sleep, stool, urine
Past History: multi-select (diseases, surgeries, vaccinations, family diseases)
Miasmatic Background: dropdown (Psora, Sycosis, Syphilis, Mixed)
Observation (Doctor): free text notes, tongue, skin, nails
Medicine Prescribed: remedy name, potency (6C/30C/200C/1M/10M/50M/CM), dose (number of drops/pills), frequency, duration
Follow-up notes linked to each visit
FR-06: Digital Prescription (Both Specialties)
Prescription header: doctor name, degree, registration number, clinic n

ame, address, date
Patient details auto-filled from record
Allopathy Rx: medicine name (with autocomplete from drug DB), strength, form, dose, frequency, duration, instructions
Homeopathy Rx: same as case paper medicine section, with additional LM/centesimal/decimal scale selector
Diagnosis field (ICD-10 code lookup for allopathy; free text for homeopathy)
One-click PDF generation; downloadable and shareable via WhatsApp
Doctor's digital signature embedded in PDF
FR-07: Billing & Payment
Consult

ation fee auto-filled from doctor's configured rate; editable per visit
Additional line items: procedures, tests, medicines sold in clinic
Payment modes: Cash or UPI (manual entry of UPI reference ID)
GST configuration (optional, for registered clinics)
Invoice generated as PDF immediately on payment confirmation
Invoice includes: clinic header, patient details, itemised charges, payment mode, total, signature
All payments logged with timestamp, mode, amount, and receptionist ID
FR-08: Automated

Post-Treatment Communications
Triggered automatically on payment confirmation:
Immediate (0 min): Thank-you message with doctor name and clinic contact
Immediate (2 min): Invoice PDF sent via WhatsApp
Immediate (5 min): Prescription PDF sent via WhatsApp
Follow-up reminder: configurable day 3, day 7, day 15 (all or selected by doctor)
Follow-up message template customisable by doctor in their language

Messages sent via Twilio WhatsApp Business API; fallback to SMS if WhatsApp unavailable
Message delivery status tracked (sent/delivered/failed)
FR-09: Income & Analytics Dashboard
Daily summary: number of patients, total collection, cash vs UPI split
Monthly/yearly earnings chart (bar graph)
Patient count trends over time
Pending follow-ups list with patient name, mobile, due date, visit type
Top medicines prescribed (homeopathy) or top diagnoses (allopathy)
Export: CSV or PDF report for any dat

e range
FR-10: Multi-Language Support
Supported languages at launch: English, Hindi (हिंदी), Marathi (मराठी)
Language preference set per user account; can be changed in settings
All static UI labels, buttons, form field names translated
Patient-facing messages (SMS/WhatsApp) sent in patient's preferred language (selectable at registration)
Case paper forms available in all supported languages
3.4 Non-Functional Requirements
4. Successful Workflow Examples
Workflow A: New Homeopathy Patient — Wal

k-in First Visit to Follow-up
Step 1 — Receptionist: Check-in & Registration
Receptionist opens MediCore → Receptionist Dashboard → 'New Patient / Walk-in'
Enters mobile number 9876543210 → System searches → No record found → 'Create New Patient' button appears
Form opens: Name (Sunita Patil), DOB (12/03/1982), Gender (Female), Blood Group (B+), Address, Allergies (none), Language preference (Marathi)
Patient created with auto-generated Patient ID: PAT-2024-0847
Appointment entry created with ty

pe = Walk-in, status = Waiting, assigned to today's date
Queue position shown: #4 (after the 3 booked appointments)
System displays estimated wait time: ~45 minutes
Step 2 — Doctor: Consultation Begins
Booked patients #1, #2, #3 complete their consultations (receptionist marks each Checked-In → In-Treatment → Completed)
Patient #4 (Sunita Patil) auto-highlighted in queue; receptionist clicks 'Call Next' → status changes to In-Treatment
Doctor's screen instantly refreshes via WebSocket push → Sho

ws Sunita Patil's full profile, chief complaint field ready
Doctor opens Homeopathy Case Paper form in Marathi interface
Step 3 — Doctor: Case Paper (Homeopathy)
Chief Complaint: Chronic headache — duration 6 months, character: throbbing, < strong light, > lying in dark room
Modalities: Worse from: noise, light, motion. Better from: rest, cold application
Mental/Emotional: Medium anxiety (dropdown: anticipatory), irritability moderate, dreams: frightening
Physical Generals: Thermal — chilly; Thi

rst: increased; Appetite: poor; Sleep: disturbed (wakes 2–3 AM)
Past History: Multi-select — Migraine (family), no surgeries; Miasmatic: Psora dominant
Doctor's observation: Pale, tongue coated white, BP 130/88
Repertorisation note (free text): Belladonna, Natrum Mur, Sanguinaria considered
Prescription: Belladonna 200C, 4 pills, thrice daily x 5 days

Doctor clicks Save Case Paper → entry created with timestamp and doctor ID
Step 4 — Billing & Payment
Doctor clicks 'Complete Consultation' → status = Awaiting Payment
Receptionist screen shows payment prompt for Sunita Patil
Consultation fee: ₹500 (pre-configured). Line item: Belladonna 200C (medicine sold) ₹80. Total: ₹580
Patient pays cash. Receptionist selects 'Cash', enters ₹580, clicks Confirm Payment
Status → Completed. Invoice PDF generated in background.
Step 5 — Automated Post-Treatmen

t Messages (Marathi)
T+0 min: WhatsApp — 'नमस्कार सुनिता ताई! डॉ. राजेश मोरे यांच्या क्लिनिकमध्ये भेट दिल्याबद्दल धन्यवाद...'
T+2 min: Invoice PDF sent via WhatsApp
T+5 min: Prescription PDF sent via WhatsApp
Day 3 (scheduled job): 'Follow-up reminder: Please update us about your health. Call: 9999999999'
Day 7 (scheduled job): Second follow-up reminder
Day 15 (scheduled job): Third follow-up reminder
Step 6 — Dashboard Update
Doctor's income dashboard: Today's earning +₹580, patient count +1
Pa

tient history shows complete visit record with case paper, Rx, payment
Follow-up tracker shows: Sunita Patil — Day 3: Pending, Day 7: Pending, Day 15: Pending
Workflow B: Returning Allopathy Patient — Appointment Booked Online, Skip & Re-queue
Step 1 — Appointment Booked in Advance
Patient or receptionist books via appointment screen: date 5-Jan, 11:00 AM slot
System sends WhatsApp confirmation: 'Appointment confirmed with Dr. Priya Kulkarni on 5-Jan at 11:00 AM. Token: #7'
Day-before reminder s

ent automatically at 6 PM: 'Reminder: Your appointment is tomorrow at 11:00 AM'
Step 2 — Day of Appointment: Patient Does Not Arrive
11:05 AM: Queue board shows Token #7 Akash Sharma in 'Scheduled' state, not yet Checked-In
11:15 AM: Receptionist notes Akash has not arrived. Walk-in patient Mr. Vinod Desai is present and waiting
Receptionist clicks 'Skip' on Akash's token → Status changes to 'No-Show (Temporary)'
Receptionist clicks 'Call Next' on Vinod Desai → Status: In-Treatment
Doctor's scre

en instantly shows Vinod Desai's records
Step 3 — Skipped Patient Arrives
11:22 AM: Akash Sharma walks in. Receptionist finds his appointment in queue
Status changed from No-Show (Temp) to Checked-In → Auto-inserted after current patient in queue
Akash is informed: 'You're next after the current patient, estimated wait: 10 minutes'
Step 4 — Doctor Consultation (Allopathy)
Vinod's consultation finishes. Doctor clicks 'Next Patient' → Akash Sharma loads
Doctor sees: Past visits (last visit 3 month

s ago), previous diagnosis (Hypertension), past prescriptions (Amlodipine 5mg)
Doctor updates diagnosis, writes new prescription on digital Rx pad:
Rx: Amlodipine 5mg — 1-0-1 — 30 days; Telmisartan 40mg — 0-0-1 — 30 days; Advice: low sodium diet, daily walk
Doctor adds follow-up: 15 days (checkbox selected)
Clicks 'Finalize Prescription' → Rx PDF generated

Step 5 — Billing (UPI)
Receptionist: Consultation fee ₹600. Patient opts for UPI
Receptionist shows QR code / UPI ID. Patient pays ₹600 via GPay
Receptionist enters UPI transaction ID: UPI-20240105-7834, clicks Confirm
Invoice generated. Status → Completed
Step 6 — Automated Messages & Follow-up
T+0: WhatsApp thank-you message in Hindi (Akash's preferred language)
T+2 min: Invoice PDF (Hindi header, amounts in INR)
T+5 min: Prescription PDF sent
Day 15 follow-up reminder scheduled. Income dashbo

ard updated: +₹600
5. Data Flow Diagrams
5.1 Context Diagram (Level 0)
The MediCore system sits at the centre, interacting with four external entities:
5.2 Level 1 DFD — Major Processes
6. Entities & Relationship Diagram (ERD)
6.1 Entity Definitions
6.2 Relationships
6.3 Key Constraints & Rules
mobile number is unique per clinic (composite unique index: clinic_id + mobile)
An appointment can have only one CasePaper and only one Prescription
Invoice is created only when appointment status = COMPL

ETED
Payment confirms the invoice; only one payment per invoice (handle refund separately)
FollowUp jobs are created on payment confirmation; cancelled if patient is No-Show
Soft delete pattern: records have deleted_at field; never hard-deleted
All monetary values stored in paise (integer) to avoid floating point errors
7. Agile Development Model
7.1 Framework: Scrum
7.2 MoSCoW Prioritisation
7.3 Testing Strategy
Unit Tests: pytest for all FastAPI route handlers, service functions, and utility f

unctions. Target: >80% coverage.
Integration Tests: Test full flows (appointment → case paper → billing → messaging pipeline) using pytest + httpx test client + test DB.
Frontend Tests: React Testing Library for component tests; Cypress for end-to-end flows.
Load Testing: Locust simulating 50 concurrent users; queue WebSocket broadcasting stress test.
UAT: Clinic receptionist and doctor use staging environment for 1 week with real patient scenarios.
Security Testing: OWASP ZAP scan, JWT token ex

piry validation, SQL injection checks via sqlmap.
7.4 API Design Conventions
8. Security & Compliance
8.1 Data Security
All API endpoints use HTTPS (TLS 1.2+); HTTP redirected to HTTPS
Patient PII (name, mobile, address) encrypted at rest using pgcrypto

Audit log table records every create/update/delete with: user_id, action, table, record_id, old_value, new_value, ip_address, timestamp
API rate limiting: 100 requests/minute per IP for public endpoints; 500 for authenticated
CORS configured to allow only frontend domain; no wildcard origins in production
